locale.__description:
Get or set the current user locale as a string, in short format, for example "en-US"
Set this property to nil to set back the locale to the current Windows user locale.
locale.__example:
print("Current language is "..sys.language)           
sys.locale = "fr-FR"
print("Current locale language is now "..sys.language)

idleThreshold.__description:
Controls how frequently the scheduler pauses to release CPU resources, defaulting to 1. A higher idleThreshold value reduces how often the scheduler pauses, prioritizing responsiveness for tasks at the cost of slightly higher CPU usage.
A lower value increases pausing frequency, reducing CPU usage but may introduce minor delays in task execution.

clipboard.__description:
Get or set data from/to the Windows clipboard. When reading, returns a table with kind and content fields, or nil if the clipboard is empty. The kind field can be "text", "files", or "image".

Notes
clipboard.__example:
local clipboard = sys.clipboard
if not clipboard then
  print("Windows clipboard is empty")
else
  print("Clipboard contains "..clipboard.kind..":")
  if clipboard.kind == "files" then
      for entry in each(clipboard.content) do
          print("\t"..entry.fullpath)
      end
      sys.clipboard = nil
  else
      print("\t"..(clipboard.content or "nil"))
  end
end

registry.delete.__returns: This function returns true if the value has been removed from the registry, or otherwise false (See sys.error to get the error message).
registry.delete.__example:
local removed = sys.registry.delete("HKEY_CURRENT_USER", "Environment", "value")
if removed then
    print("Removed registry key")
else
   print("Failed to remove key")
end

__params.__description:
Indicates the predefined entry point in the Windows registry, from which the key/value will be accessed. Must be one of the following string :

  • "HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT" : types (or classes) of documents and the properties associated with those types
  • "HKEY_CURRENT_CONFIG" : information about the current hardware
  • "HKEY_CURRENT_USER" : preferences of the current user
  • "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE" : data about the computer, system memory, and installed hardware and software
Indicates the name of the key to be deleted, or the path to the value to be deleted (the value must be provided as a third parameter).
Indicates the specific value to be deleted, removes all values for the key if omitted

registry.delete.__description: Remove a key and all it's assosciated values, or just a value, from the Windows registry.
registry.delete.__tinydesc: Deletes a value from the Windows registry

atexit.__description: The sys.atexit property get or set the function to be called at program exit. This function don't take any arguments.
atexit.__example:
sys.atexit = function()
   print("You are leaving LuaRT !")
end

--- Very long sleep, press CTRL+C to force program exit (and call sys.atexit function)
sleep(100000)
atexit.__tinydesc: Get/set the function to call at program exit

__description: The sys module provides access to system-specific parameters and functions

contains.__returns: Returns a number representing the byte sequence position in the Buffer, or false if it has not been found.
contains.__example:
local buff1 = sys.Buffer { 97, 98, 99 }    -- "abc" in utf8
local buff2 = sys.Buffer { 100, 101, 102 } -- "def" in utf8

-- outputs false
print(buff1:contains(buff2))

__params.__description: A string or Buffer value that contains the byte sequence to search for.

contains.__description: Checks if the Buffer contains the whole byte pattern.
contains.__tinydesc: Search for bytes pattern in the buffer

unpack.__example:
-- create a Buffer with 3 bytes
local b = sys.Buffer({1, 1, 21})

-- deserialize values from bytes
local day, month, year = b:unpack("BBB")

print("Today is "..day.."/"..month.."/"..year)

__params.__description: A format string to specify the binary layout to serialize the values. See Lua programming reference "string.pack" for available sequence of conversions options.

unpack.__description: Deserialize the Buffer, using the provided format.

size.__description:
The Buffer.size property get or set the current Buffer byte size. The property permits to extend or shrink the Buffer.
The length operator # can be used to get the Buffer size
size.__example:
-- init a Buffer with a size of 4 zero-bytes
local b = sys.Buffer(4)

-- outputs "4"
print(b.size)

-- pack 3 integers (4 bytes each) in the buffer, and resize it automagically
b:pack("I4I4I4", 0x1111, 0x2222, 0x3333)

-- outputs "12"
print(b.size)
size.__tinydesc: Get the buffer length in bytes

Buffer iteration.__description: Buffers are iterable through the Luart helper function each(), returning the next byte in the Buffer with each loop iteration. Each iteration returns the byte value as a number.
Buffer iteration.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

console.write("\nEnter your name : ")
local buff = sys.Buffer(console.readln())
console.write("\nLet me show you its binary representation... ")

for byte in each(buff) do
    local hex = string.format("%X", byte)
    console.write("["..hex.."] ")
end
console.write("\n")
Buffer iteration.__tinydesc: Iterate over the buffer, byte by byte

from.__example:
-- init a Buffer with one byte
local buff = sys.Buffer(1)

-- update buff content with a string
buff:from("This is a string");
print(buff) --Outputs This is a string

__params.__description:
A variable used to initialize the Buffer. Only number, string and table values are permitted with the following behavior :

  • number : Initialize the Buffer with the specified size. All bytes are initialized to zero.
  • string : Initialize the Buffer with the specified string. An optional encoding argument can specify how to interpret the string (see below)
  • table : Initialize the Buffer with the specified table. The table must contain only sequential byte values (for example { 20, 33, 75 })

An optional string used to specify the encoding when initializing the Buffer with a string value :

  • "utf8" : Initialize the Buffer with a UTF8 string encoding, or as raw binary data (default)
  • "unicode" : Initialize the Buffer with an UNICODE (UCS-2 LE) encoded string
  • "base64" : Initialize the Buffer with a base64 encoded string
  • "hex" : Initialize the Buffer with a hexadecimal encoded string

from.__description: Reinitialize the Buffer with the specified content.
from.__tinydesc: Reinitialize the buffer with new data

decompress.__example:
local text = [[Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed non risus. Suspendisse lectus 
tortor, dignissim sit amet, adipiscing nec, ultricies sed, dolor. Cras elementum ultrices diam. Maecenas
ligula massa, varius a, semper congue, euismod non, mi. Proin porttitor, orci nec nonummy 
molestie, enim est eleifend mi, non fermentum diam nisl sit amet erat.]]

local compressed = sys.Buffer(text):compress()

-- outputs true
print(tostring(compressed:decompress()) == text)
decompress.__description: Decompress the Buffer to another binary Buffer, using the DEFLATE decompression algorithm.
decompress.__tinydesc: Decompress the buffer using DEFLATE algorithm

Buffer.__tinydesc: Object that represent a block of memory

Buffer equality comparison.__description: Buffer equality allows to compare buffers. A buffer will be equal to another when their sizes and contents are the same.

Buffer[].__description:
Buffer indexing allow to get/set individual byte value.

  • The index value must be an integer.
  • First byte of the Buffer starts at index 1.
  • Negative index means counting from the end of the Buffer (for example buffer[-1] means the last byte)
Buffer[].__example:
local buff = sys.Buffer { 97, 98, 99 } -- a, b, c in UTF8

-- change last byte of the buffer
buff[-1] = 67

-- outputs "abC"
print(buff)

-- outputs 67
print(buff[-1])
Buffer[].__tinydesc: Index the buffer by byte position

append.__example:
-- init a Buffer with one byte
local buff = sys.Buffer("AB")
buff:append("CD")

-- outputs "ABCD"
print(buff)

__params.__description:
A variable used to append data the Buffer. Only number, string and table values are permitted with the following behavior :

  • number : Append the Buffer with the specified size. All the appended bytes are initialized to zero.
  • string : Append the Buffer with the specified string. An optional encoding argument can specify how to interpret the string (see below)
  • table : Append the Buffer with the specified table. The table must contain only sequential byte values (for example { 20, 33, 75 })

An optional string used to specify the encoding when appending the Buffer with a string value :

  • "utf8" : Append the Buffer with a UTF8 string encoding, or as raw binary data (default)
  • "unicode" : Append the Buffer with an UNICODE (UCS-2 LE) encoded string
  • "base64" : Append the Buffer with a base64 encoded string
  • "hex" : Append the Buffer with a hexadecimal encoded string

append.__description: Append binary data to the Buffer with the specified content.

__params.__description:
A string that specifies the encoding method to convert the binary Buffer to string :

  • "utf8" : uses the UTF8 encoding (default)
  • "unicode" : uses the UNICODE (UCS-2 LE) encoding
  • "base64" : uses base64 encoding
  • "hex" : uses hexadecimal encoding

encode.__description: Returns a string, encoded from the Buffer according to the provided encoding.

Buffer to string conversion.__description:
Buffer can be converted to a string using the standard Lua function tostring().

Please use the Buffer:encode() method to convert the Buffer data with another encoding, or change the Buffer encoding.
Buffer to string conversion.__example:
-- create a Buffer from string encoded with base64
local buff = sys.Buffer("TMO7w6Q=", "base64")

-- outputs true
print(tostring(buff:encode('utf8')) == "Lûä")
Buffer to string conversion.__tinydesc: Convert the buffer to a string

compress.__example:
local text = [[Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed non risus. Suspendisse lectus 
tortor, dignissim sit amet, adipiscing nec, ultricies sed, dolor. Cras elementum ultrices diam. Maecenas
ligula massa, varius a, semper congue, euismod non, mi. Proin porttitor, orci nec nonummy 
molestie, enim est eleifend mi, non fermentum diam nisl sit amet erat.]]

local compressed = sys.Buffer(text):compress()

print("size of text in bytes : "..#text)
print("size of compressed buffer in bytes : "..#compressed)
print("compression ratio : "..tostring(math.floor(100-#compressed/#text * 100)).."%")
compress.__description: Compress the Buffer to another binary Buffer, using the DEFLATE compression algorithm.
compress.__tinydesc: Compress the buffer using DEFLATE algorithm

Buffer length operator.__description: The length operator # applied on a Buffer object returns the total number of bytes it contains.
Buffer length operator.__example:
local buff1 = sys.Buffer { 97, 98, 99 }    -- "abc" in utf8
local buff2 = sys.Buffer { 100, 101, 102 } -- "def" in utf8

local concat = buff1..buff2

-- outputs 6
print(#concat)
Buffer length operator.__tinydesc: Get the size of the Buffer in bytes

pack.__example:
local date = sys.Datetime()

local b = sys.Buffer(1)

-- serialize date fields in bytes
b:pack("BBB", date.day, date.month, date.year-2000)

-- outputs 3
print(b.size)

print("Today is "..b[1].."/"..b[2].."/20"..b[3])

__params.__description: A format string to specify the binary layout to serialize the values. See Lua programming reference "string.pack" for available sequence of conversions options.
One or more values to serialize in binary format.

pack.__description: Reinitialize the Buffer with serialized values.
pack.__tinydesc: Reinitialize the buffer with serialized data

encoding.__description: The Buffer.encoding property get or set the encoding to be used when converting the Buffer to a string
encoding.__example:
-- init a Buffer with the unicode string "Héllo"
local buff = sys.Buffer("Héllo", "unicode")

-- prints the buffer as a string using "unicode" encoding
print(buff.encoding, "=>", buff)

-- change buffer encoding to "utf8"
buff.encoding = "utf8"

-- prints the buffer as a string using "utf8" encoding
print(buff.encoding, "=>", buff)
encoding.__tinydesc: Get/set the buffer encoding for string conversion

Buffer.__description: The Buffer object holds and manages raw binary data

sub.__returns: Returns a Buffer value.
sub.__example:
-- init a Buffer with a string
local b = sys.Buffer("ABC")

-- returns a sub Buffer from b[2] until the end
local newbuff = b:sub(2)

-- outputs "BC"
print(newbuff)

__params.__description: An index of the Buffer defining the start of the sub-Buffer. That index can be negative (starting to count from the end of the Buffer).

An optional index that defines where the sub-Buffer ends. That index can be negative (starting to count from the end of the Buffer).
When that argument is absent, it is assumed to be equal to -1 (end of the Buffer).

sub.__description: Extracts part of the Buffer like the function string.sub.
sub.__tinydesc: Extracts part of the bytes from the buffer

__params.__description:
A variable used to initialize the Buffer. Only number, string and table values are permitted with the following behavior :

  • number : Initialize the Buffer with the specified size. All bytes are initialized to zero.
  • string : Initialize the Buffer with the specified string. An optional encoding argument can specify how to decode the string (see below)
  • table : Initialize the Buffer with the specified table. The table must contain only sequential byte values (for example { 20, 33, 75 })

An optional string used to specify the decoding method when initializing the Buffer with a string value :

  • "utf8" : Initialize the Buffer with a UTF8 encoded string, or as raw binary data (default)
  • "unicode" : Initialize the Buffer with an UNICODE (UCS-2 LE) encoded string
  • "base64" : Initialize the Buffer with a base64 encoded string
  • "hex" : Initialize the Buffer with a hexadecimal encoded string

constructor.__description:
The Buffer constructor returns a Buffer value representing a sequence of raw bytes in memory, initialized with the var argument. The Buffer value can then be accessed, modified, resized, converted from/to string, compressed, decoded...
Prefer to use Buffer instead of strings when manipulating binary data. Remember that Luart strings are UTF8 encoded.
constructor.__example:
-- initialize with a table, sequence of bytes values
local b = sys.Buffer( {0x41, 0, 0x42, 0, 0x43, 0} )

-- decode the Buffer as a unicode string and prints it (outputs "ABC")
print(b:encode("unicode"))

Buffer concatenation.__description: Buffer concatenation allow to append two buffers to each other, or to concatenate a Buffer with a string (using its encoding). The result of the concatenation is a third Buffer.
Buffer concatenation.__example:
local buff1 = sys.Buffer { 97, 98, 99 }    -- "abc" in utf8
local buff2 = sys.Buffer { 100, 101, 102 } -- "def" in utf8

local concat1 = buff1..buff2

-- outputs 6
print(#concat1)
Buffer concatenation.__tinydesc: Concatenate the buffer with another one or with a string

hour.__description:
The Datetime.hour property returns a number representing the hour of the moment. Valid values are from 0 through 23.
Setting the hour property with a value lower or higher will throw an error.
hour.__example:
-- prints the current hour, for example "12"
print(sys.Datetime().hour)

format.__returns: This function returns the formatted string.
format.__example:
-- get current date and time
local today = sys.Datetime()

-- prints only the current time, not the date
print("Current time = "..today:format(nil, "HH:mm:ss"))

__params.__description:
An optional string representing the format for the date part. For only formatting the time part, use nil in place. The format tokens accepted are :

  • Year : "y" or "yy" for short format of the Year, and "yyyy" for the long format.
  • Month :"M" or "MM" for short format of the Month, and "MMMM" for the long format.
  • Day : "d" or "dd" for short format of the Day, and "dddd" for the long format.

An optional string representing the format for the time part. For only formatting the date part, omit that argument. The format tokens accepted are :

  • Hours (12-hour clock): "h", or "hh" with a leading zero.
  • Hours (24-hour clock): "H, or "HH" with aleading zero.
  • Minutes :"m" or "mm" with a leading zero.
  • Seconds : "s" or "ss" with a leading zero
  • Time marker : "t or "tt" for AM/PM time marker.

format.__description: Formats the Datetime value as a string.

weekday.__description:
The Datetime.weekday property returns a number representing the day of the week. Valid values are from 1 through 7.
  1. Sunday
  2. Monday
  3. Tuesday
  4. Wednesday
  5. Thursday
  6. Friday
  7. Saturday
Setting the weekday property with a value lower or higher will throw an error.
weekday.__example:
-- prints the current day of the week, for example "3" for Thursday
print(sys.Datetime().weekday)

day.__description:
The Datetime.day property returns a number representing the day of the month. Valid values are from 1 through 31.
Setting the day property with a value lower or higher will throw an error.
day.__example:
-- prints the current day, for example "1" for the first day of the month
print(sys.Datetime().day)
day.__tinydesc: Get/set the day of the month

time.__description: The Datetime.time property returns a string representing the "time part" of the moment, in the current local short format.
time.__example:
-- get the current local date and time
local dt = sys.Datetime()

-- prints for example "12:00:01"
print(dt.time)
time.__tinydesc: Get the time as a string

__params.__description: An optional string used to specify the Datetime value, using the current short date format depending on the user current locale.

constructor.__description:
The Datetime constructor returns a Datetime value representing a moment, including a date part and a time part.
The Datetime value is initialized with the moment argument. If that argument is omitted, the constructor returns the current local date and time. The Datetime value can then be accessed, modified, converted to string,...
The format string depends on the current user locale, using the standard short format.
For example, for the date part, the constructor use mm/dd/yyyy for English but dd/mm/yyyy for French locale

Please note that using the Datetime constructor, the maximum allowed value for the year is 9999. If needed, you can change the year to a higher value with an assignment to the year property (which allows a maximum value of 30827)
constructor.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

-- get current date and time
local today = sys.Datetime()

-- get the year of birth from the user
console.write("Enter your year of birth : ")
local yearofbirth = tonumber(console.readln())

-- ouputs the age of the user
print("You are "..tonumber(today.year-yearofbirth).." old")

Datetime.__tinydesc: Object representation of a moment in time

minute.__description:
The Datetime.minute property returns a number representing the minutes of the moment. Valid values are from 0 through 59.
Setting the minute property with a value lower or higher will throw an error.
minute.__example:
-- prints the current minute, for example "30"
print(sys.Datetime().minute)

milliseconds.__description:
The Datetime.milliseconds property returns a number representing the milliseconds of the moment. Valid values are from 0 through 999.
Setting the milliseconds property with a value lower or higher will throw an error.
milliseconds.__example:
-- prints the current number of milliseconds, for example "356"
print(sys.Datetime().milliseconds)

second.__description:
The Datetime.second property returns a number representing the second of the moment. Valid values are from 0 through 59.
Setting the second property with a value lower or higher will throw an error.
second.__example:
-- prints the current number of seconds, for example "59"
print(sys.Datetime().second)

year.__description:
The Datetime.year property returns a number representing the year of the moment. Valid values are from 1601 through 30827.
Setting the year property with a value lower or higher will throw an error.
year.__example:
-- prints the current year, for example "2021"
print(sys.Datetime().year)

month.__description:
The Datetime.month property returns a number representing the month of the moment. Valid values are from 1 through 12.
Setting the month property with a value lower or higher will throw an error.
  1. January
  2. February
  3. March
  4. April
  5. May
  6. June
  7. July
  8. August
  9. September
  10. October
  11. November
  12. December
month.__example:
-- prints the current month, for example "1" for January
print(sys.Datetime().month)

interval.__returns: Return a number as an interval, expressed in days by default.
interval.__example:
-- get current date and time
local today = sys.Datetime()

-- prints only the current time, not the date
print("Current time = "..today:format(nil, "HH:mm:ss"))

__params.__description: A Datetime value from where the interval will be calculated, to the current Datetime value.

An optional string that indicates how the interval will be expressed (by default, in days). The following strings are accepted :

  • "seconds"
  • "minutes"
  • "hours"
  • "days"
  • "months"
  • "years"

interval.__description: Returns an interval with the from Datetime value, expressed by default in days.

date.__description: The Datetime.date property returns a string representing the "date part" of the moment, in the current local short format.
date.__example:
-- get the current local date and time
local dt = sys.Datetime()

-- prints for example "01/01/2021"
print(dt.date)
date.__tinydesc: Get the date as a string

Datetime.__description: The Datetime object handles dates and times

monthname.__description: The Datetime.monthname property returns a string representing the name of the month of the moment, in the current local.
monthname.__example:
-- prints the current local name of the month, for example "january"
print(sys.Datetime().monthname)
monthname.__tinydesc: Get the month name as a string

dayname.__description: The Datetime.dayname property returns a string representing the name of the day of the moment, in the current local.
dayname.__example:
-- prints the current local name of the day, for example "monday"
print(sys.Datetime().dayname)
dayname.__tinydesc: Get the day name as a string

registry.write.__returns: This function returns true if the value has been written to the registry, or otherwise false (See sys.error to get the error message).
registry.write.__example:
-- Get the current user PATH environment variable
local user_path = sys.registry.read("HKEY_CURRENT_USER", "Environment", "Path", false) or ""

-- Append it with a new folder "D:\\mypath\\bin\\"
sys.registry.write("HKEY_CURRENT_USER", "Environment", "Path", user_path..";".."D:\\mypath\\bin\\", true)

__params.__description:
Indicates the predefined entry point in the Windows registry, from which the key/value will be accessed. Must be one of the following string :

  • "HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT" : types (or classes) of documents and the properties associated with those types
  • "HKEY_CURRENT_CONFIG" : information about the current hardware
  • "HKEY_CURRENT_USER" : preferences of the current user
  • "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE" : data about the computer, system memory, and installed hardware and software
Indicates the name of the key under which the value will be written.
Indicates the name of the value.

Indicates the content of the value according to the following conversion rules :
Lua value typeRegistry value type
BufferREG_BINARY
numberREG_QWORD
REG_DWORD
stringREG_SZ
REG_EXPAND_SZ
table of stringsREG_MULTI_SZ
nilREG_NONE
Indicates if the environment variables should be expanded (for strings only), defaults to false

registry.write.__description: Write a value under the specified key in the Windows registry.
registry.write.__tinydesc: Write a value in the Windows registry

__params.__description: A string representing the class name of the Windows COM object. The class name must correspond to a Windows registered COM component.

An optional boolean, that when set to true, indicates that an already running instance of the COM object will be used. If set to false (the default), the system will create a new instance of the COM object.

When using Luart x64 interpreter, the class may not exists as some Windows COM objects are only 32bits compatible.

constructor.__description: The COM constructor returns a COM object instance, representing an abstraction of a Windows COM object.
constructor.__example:
local dbname = "data.mdb"

-- Create a ADOX.Catalog COM object
local cat = sys.COM("ADOX.Catalog")

-- Call the "Create" method of ADOX.Catalog object to create a _Connection (another COM Object) to the database file
local con = cat:Create("Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;Jet OLEDB:Engine Type=5;Data Source="..dbname)

COM Object constants.__description: Constants associated with a COM Object can be accessed by indexing the COM object with the constant name.
COM Object constants.__example:
local word = sys.COM("Word.Application")
local doc = word.Documents:Add()
local sel = word.Selection

-- Set the Seleciton.Paragraphs.Alignment property to wdAlignParagraphCenter
sel.Paragraphs.Alignment = word.wdAlignParagraphCenter

COM.__description: The COM object instantiate and controls Windows applications and components

COM Object iteration.__description:
COM Objects are iterable through the Luart> helper function each(), returning the next method/property in the COM object at each loop iteration.
Each iteration returns two strings, a field name and its type, which can be one of those values :

  • "method": the field is a method that can be called.
  • "get property": the field is a property that can be get.
  • "set property": the field is a property that can be set.
COM Object iteration.__example:
-- Create a FileSystemObject COM component
local fso = sys.COM("Scripting.FileSystemObject")

-- Iterate over it to get its fields
for field, kind in each(fso) do
    print(field..":", kind)
end
COM Object iteration.__tinydesc: COM Objects can be iterated over

COM.__tinydesc: Object that represent a Windows COM Object

beep.__description: Plays a beeping sound of 550hz for a duration of 0.2 seconds (200 milliseconds).

wait.__returns: This method returns the Task return values.
wait.__example:
-- example that will draw french flags concurrently

import console

-- function to print a colored square to the console forever
local function draw(color)
  while true do
    -- sleep for one second
    sleep(1000)
    -- and print a colored square
    console.writecolor(color, "■")
    if color == "red" then
        console.write("\n")
    end
  end
end

-- Launch 3 task using the point() function
async(draw, "blue")
async(draw, "white")
async(draw, "red")

-- Wait for all tasks to terminate before the program exits
waitall()
wait.__description: Suspend the execution flow until the Task have been terminated, allowing other tasks to run during this time.
wait.__tinydesc: Wait for the task to terminate

pause.__example:
local t = sys.Task(function()
    for i = 1, 10 do
        print(("task: step %d"):format(i))
        sleep(500) -- yield to scheduler
    end
    print("task: completed")
end)

t()                -- start task
sleep(1200)        -- let task run a couple of steps

print("main: pausing task")
t:pause()          -- pause the task
print("main: task paused, sleeping 2000ms")
sleep(2000)        -- task remains paused during this time

print("main: resuming task")
t:resume()         -- resume the task
await nt           -- wait for completion
print("main: task.status =", t.status)
pause.__description: Suspend the Task execution until the Task is resumed with Task:resume().

resume.__example:
local t = sys.Task(function()
    for i = 1, 10 do
        print(("task: step %d"):format(i))
        sleep(500) -- yield to scheduler
    end
    print("task: completed")
end)

t()                -- start task
sleep(1200)        -- let task run a couple of steps

print("main: pausing task")
t:pause()          -- pause the task
print("main: task paused, sleeping 2000ms")
sleep(2000)        -- task remains paused during this time

print("main: resuming task")
t:resume()         -- resume the task
await nt           -- wait for completion
print("main: task.status =", t.status)
resume.__description: Resume the Task execution, previously paused by Task:pause().

status.__description:
The Task.status returns the current state of the Task, as a string. Possible values are :
  • "created" : the Task has just been created, and has not started.
  • "running" : the Task is currently running.
  • "paused" : the Task has been paused.
  • "sleeping" : the Task is sleeping (ie has called the global sleep() function).
  • "waiting" : the Task is waiting for another Task to terminate.
  • "terminated" : the Task has terminated its execution.
status.__example:
-- print a message after a delay (in seconds)
local function print_after(delay, message)
    sleep(delay*1000)
    print(message)
end

-- print "Hello" after 1 second
local task1 = sys.Task(print_after)
print("Task1 is "..task1.status)
task1(1, "Hello")

-- print "Hello" after 2 seconds
local task2 = sys.Task(print_after)
print("Task2 is "..task2.status)
task2(1, "Hello")

await(task1)
print("Task1 is "..task1.status)
await(task2)
print("Task2 is "..task2.status)

-- wait for all tasks to finish before exit
waitall()

priority.__description: The Task.priority property get/set the Task execution priority, a number. Tasks with high priority values will be resumed before others by the scheduler
priority.__example:
local N = 500

local finished = {}

local low = sys.Task(function()
    for i = 1, N do sleep() end
    finished[#finished+1] = "low"
end)

local high = sys.Task(function()
    for i = 1, N do sleep() end
    finished[#finished+1] = "high"
end)

low.priority  = 0   -- lower priority
high.priority = 2   -- higher priority

low()   -- start low
high()  -- start high

waitall(high, low)

print("finish order:", table.concat(finished, ", "))

timeout.__description: The Task.timeout property get/set the Task execution timeout in milliseconds. Once the timeout is reached, the Task will be terminated.
timeout.__example:
local t = sys.Task(function()
    print("task: started, will sleep 5000ms")
    sleep(5000) -- long work
    print("task: finished (this may not run if timed out)")
end)

-- set a 1 second timeout (milliseconds)
t.timeout = 1000

-- start and wait
t()
await nt

print("task.status:", t.status)     -- "terminated" expected
print("task.expired:", t.expired)   -- true if terminated due to timeout

expired.__description: The Task.expired property returns true if the Task.timeout is reached, or false otherwise.
expired.__example:
local t = sys.Task(function()
    print("task: started, will sleep 5000ms")
    sleep(5000) -- long work
    print("task: finished (this may not run if timed out)")
end)

-- set a 1 second timeout (milliseconds)
t.timeout = 1000

-- start and wait
t()
await nt

print("task.status:", t.status)     -- "terminated" expected
print("task.expired:", t.expired)   -- true if terminated due to timeout
expired.__tinydesc: Check if the current task expired

Task calling.__description:
To start a just created Task instance, uses the parenthesis notation, as for function calling.

The provided arguments will be passed to the Task function.
Task calling.__example:
-- Counting concurrently

import console

local function count(start, color)
    local i = start  
    while true do
    -- sleep for one second
    sleep(500)
    -- and print the number with the provided color
    console.writecolor(color, i, "\n")
    i = i + 2
    end
end

-- Create 2 Task using the count() function
local task1 = sys.Task(count)
local task2 = sys.Task(count)

-- Start first task starting to count from 1 in blue
task1(1, "blue")
-- Start first task starting to count from 2 in red
task2(2, "red")

-- Wait for all tasks to terminate before the program exits
waitall()
Task calling.__tinydesc: Start a task by calling it as a function

after.__description:
This property is used to define the function to be called once the Task is terminated.
When set, this function is called with the Task return values as arguments.
after.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import net

-- The Http:get() method returns a Task value
local client = net.Http("https://wttr.in"):get("/Paris?format=3")

-- Set the client.after property, which take two arguments (the client and the response table)
function client:after(response)
    print(response.content)
end

-- This could have been written this way too :
-- net.Http("https://wttr.in"):get("/Paris?format=3").after = function(client, response)
--     print(response.content)
-- end

waitall()
after.__tinydesc: Get/set the function to be called once the Task is terminated

terminated.__description:
Checks if the Task has terminated its execution, returning true or false otherwise.
  • "created" : the Task has just been created, and has not started.
  • "running" : the Task is currently running.
  • "sleeping" : the Task is sleeping (ie has called the global sleep() function).
  • "waiting" : the Task is waiting for another Task to terminate.
  • "terminated" : the Task has terminated its execution.
terminated.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui

local win = ui.Window("Task.terminated sample", 320, 200)

local pb = ui.Progressbar(win)
pb:center()

local task = async(function()
    while pb.position < 100 do
        pb:advance(1)
        sleep()
    end
end)

local uitask = await win:showasync()

while not task.terminated do
    win:status("Position = "..pb.position)
    sleep()
end
terminated.__tinydesc: Check if the task is terminated

Task.__description: The Task object runs functions in parallel to the main flow

__params.__description:
A function that will be executed once the Task is started.

To start a Task, just call it like a function, see Task calling

constructor.__description: The Task constructor returns an asynchronous Task, executing the provided function when started.
constructor.__example:
-- print a message after a delay (in seconds)
local function print_after(delay, message)
  sleep(delay*1000)
  print(message)
end

-- creates a Task to print "Hello" after 1 second, and starts it
local task1 = sys.Task(print_after)
task1(1, "Hello")

-- creates a Task to print "Hello" after 1 second, and starts it
local task2 = sys.Task(print_after)
task2(1, "Hello")

-- wait for all tasks to finish before exit
waitall()

Task.__tinydesc: Object that represent an asynchronous task

cancel.__returns: This method returns true, or false if the Task was already terminated.
cancel.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui

local win = ui.Window("Task:cancel() sample", 320, 200)

local pb1 = ui.Progressbar(win)
local pb2 = ui.Progressbar(win)

pb1:center()
pb1.y = pb1.y - 20
pb2:center()
pb2.y = pb2.y + 20

pb1.themed = false
pb1.fgcolor = 0xEFB42C
pb2.themed = false
pb2.fgcolor = 0x000050

local btn = ui.Button(win, "Stop the Task")
btn:center()
btn.y = btn.y + 60

local function who_wins()
    ui.info(pb1.position >= 100 and "Orange progress bar wins !" or "Blue progress bar wins !")
    win:hide()
end

local task = async(function()
    while pb1.position < 100 or pb2.position < 100 do
        pb1:advance(math.random(1, 10))
        pb2:advance(math.random(1, 10))
        sleep(500)
    end
    who_wins()
end)

function btn:onClick()
    task:cancel()
    who_wins()
end

await win:showasync()
cancel.__description: Stops Task execution, and set its status to "terminated".

cmd.__returns: This function returns true if the command was sucessfully executed or false otherwise.
cmd.__example:
-- open an explorer window from the current directory
sys.cmd('start .')

__params.__description: A string that contains the command to send to the command interpreter.
An optional boolean value indicating if the command must be executed in a hidden window (the default).

An optional boolean value indicating whether the child process should be independent from the current process. In this case, when the current process is stopped, the child process will be able to continue its execution. Default value is false.

tempfile.__returns: Returns a File Object that represents a temporary file located in the Windows system temporary path. In case of error, this function returns nil
tempfile.__example:
local temp = sys.tempfile()
print("Temporary file path: "..temp.fullpath)

-- copy the temporary file to the current directory
temp:copy(".\\config.ini")

__params.__description: An optional string that contains the first 3 letters of the temporary filename.

tempfile.__description: Create a temporary empty File Object, created in the user temporary folder.
tempfile.__tinydesc: Generate a temporary File object in Windows temporary folder

halt.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

print('Do you want to close the current session ? (y/n)')
local answer = string.upper(console.read(1))
if answer == 'Y' then
    sys.halt("logoff")
end

__params.__description:
An optional string that indicates the shutdown method to use. If this parameter is not specified, the default value "shutdown" is used.

  • "logoff" : close the current Windows session
  • "shutdown" : shutdown the computer
  • "reboot" : reboot the computer

halt.__tinydesc: Logoff, shutdown or reboot the computer

tasks.__returns: Returns a table containing all currently active Task objects.
tasks.__example:
for task in each(sys.tasks) do
	print(task.status)
end
tasks.__description: Get all currently active Task objects.

TaskFactory.__returns: Returns a factory function that can be used to create new Task at each call.
TaskFactory.__example:
local factory = sys.TaskFactory(function(msg, delay)
	sleep(delay)
	print(msg)
end)

-- Create and start two tasks
factory("Hello", 1000)
factory("World", 2000)

__params.__description: A function to be used as the Task main function.

exit.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

print('Do you want to terminate the program ? (y/n)')
local answer = string.upper(console.read(1))
if answer == 'Y' then
    sys.exit()
end

__params.__description: An optionnal number returned to the operating system. Default value is zero. By convention, a value of zero means that the program completed successfully. A value of -1 means that an error occured.

__tinydesc: Provides access to system-level functions : files, folders, Windows registry...

error.__description: The sys.error property returns a string representing the last Windows error message or nil if no error occured.
error.__example:
-- copy a file that does not exist
local file = sys.File("xjkyezzrkjzkf")
if file:copy("newfile.bin") == false then
	print("error : "..sys.error)
end
error.__tinydesc: Get the last Windows error message

language.__description: Get the full localized name of the current locale as a string, for example "english (United States)"
language.__example:
print("Current user locale is "..sys.locale)           
print("Current user language is "..sys.language)
language.__tinydesc: Get the full localized name of the current locale

registry.read.__returns:
This function returns nil if the value cannot be found, or returns the registry value according to the following conversion rules :
Registry value type Lua value type
REG_BINARY Buffer
REG_QWORD
REG_DWORD		 number
REG_SZ
REG_EXPAND_SZ		 string
REG_LINK string
REG_MULTI8SZ table of strings
REG_NONE nil
registry.read.__example:
local cpu = sys.registry.read("HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE", "HARDWARE\\DESCRIPTION\\System\\CentralProcessor\\0", "ProcessorNameString")
print("[ CPU ]", cpu)

__params.__description:
Indicates the predefined entry point in the Windows registry, from which the key/value will be accessed. Must be one of the following string :

  • "HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT" : types (or classes) of documents and the properties associated with those types
  • "HKEY_CURRENT_CONFIG" : information about the current hardware
  • "HKEY_CURRENT_USER" : preferences of the current user
  • "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE" : data about the computer, system memory, and installed hardware and software
Indicates the name of the key with which the value is associated.

Indicates if environment strings should be expanded or not (defaults to false). Registry values may contains references to environment variables (for example, %PATH%). Expanding those references replaces them with their current value.

registry.read.__description: Read a value associated with the provided key, in the Windows registry.
registry.read.__tinydesc: Read a value from the Windows registry

env.__description: Get a proxy table representing environment variables. To set or get an environment variable, just index the table with the variable name.
env.__example:
-- get the current Windows installation path
print("Windows installation path: "..sys.env["windir"])

-- set the local environment variable "root"
sys.env["root"] = "C:\\"
print("root environment variable: "..sys.env["root"])

clock.__returns: This function returns the current elapsed time in millisecond. Lua<sup>rt</sup> uses a high performance counter if available, or the current elapsed time since the system has started otherwise.
clock.__example:
function benchmark(name, func, ...)
	local start = sys.clock()
	func(...)
	local finish = sys.clock()
	print(name.." benchmark : "..tostring(finish-start).."ms")
end

function fibonacci(n)
  local a, b = 0, 1
  for i = 1, n do
    a, b = b, a + b
  end
  return a
end

benchmark("fibonacci", fibonacci, 5000000)
clock.__description: Returns the current elapsed time, that can be used for time-interval measurements.
clock.__tinydesc: Get elapsed time from high performance counter

hidden.__description: The File.hidden property set or get a boolean value indicating if the File is hidden or not. The file must exist physically on the disk.
hidden.__example:
local file = sys.File("example\\notepad.wlua")
file.hidden = true

-- outputs "true"
print(file.hidden)
hidden.__tinydesc: Check if the file is hidden

name.__description: The File.name property returns a string representing the file name and extension of the File.
name.__tinydesc: Get the File name (with its extension)

File.__description: The File object handles reading, writing, and managing files on disk

position.__description:
The File.position property get or set the current file position, as a number. The file must have been opened before using this property.
File position always starts at 1. File position interpretation depends on the file encoding.
  • In "binary" encoding : the position is expressed in bytes.
  • In "utf8" or "unicode" encoding : the position is expressed in characters (and you don't have to care about BOM).
position.__example:
local file = sys.File("unicode.txt")
	
file:open("write", "unicode")
file:write("Hello World !")
file:close()
file:open("read")
file.position = 8

-- read one character starting at position 8 : outputs "o"
print("["..file:read(1).."]")

close.__example:
local file = sys.File("Hello.txt")

-- open the file for writing, using default utf8 encoding
file:open("write")

file:write("Hello World")
file:close()
close.__description: Closes the file previously opened for read/write operations. Any further read/write operations will fail.

readonly.__description: The File.readonly property set or get a boolean value indicating if the File is readonly or not. The file must exist physically on the disk.
readonly.__example:
local file = sys.File("example\\notepad.wlua")
file.readonly = true

-- outputs "true"
print(file.readonly)
readonly.__tinydesc: Check if the file is readonly

remove.__returns: Returns true if the operation succeeded or false otherwise (in case of an opened file for example).
remove.__example:
local file = sys.File("text.txt"):open("write")
file:writeln("Lorem ipsum")

-- output false : file is open and cannot be removed
print(file:remove())
remove.__description: Deletes the file physically on the disk.

directory.__description: The File.directory property returns a Directory representing the folder that contains the file.
directory.__example:
local interpreter = sys.File(arg[-1])

-- prints interpreter executable ("luart.exe") directory
print(interpreter.directory.fullpath)
directory.__tinydesc: Get the directory that contains the file

writeln.__returns: Returns the number of bytes written to the file without the end of line character.
writeln.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

-- write "Hello World !\n" to the console.stdout standard File
console.stdout:writeln("Hello World !")

__params.__description: A variable converted to a string value to be written to the file.

writeln.__description: Writes data to the file, adding a terminal end of line character.
writeln.__tinydesc: Write data and append a new line to the file

read.__returns: Returns the data as a string (for utf8 and unicode encoding) or as a Buffer (for binary encoding).
read.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

console.write("Do you want to quit ? (y/n) ")

-- read one character from console.stdin standard File
local c = console.stdin:read(1)

if c == "y" then
	print("Bye !")
else
	print("Let's continue...")
end
read.__results: string or Buffer

__params.__description:
An optional number indicating the number of characters (or bytes in binary encoding) to read.
When no value is provided, the method read all data from the current position until the end of file is reached.

modified.__description:
The File.modified property set or get a Datetime value indicating when the file has been last modified. The file must exist physically on the disk.
If the file do not exist, this property returnsniland setting its value will have no effect.
modified.__example:
local file = sys.File("newfile.txt"):open("write")
file:write("This is a test file")
file:close()

local timestamp = sys.Datetime("01/01/1970")
file.modified = timestamp

-- outputs "true"
print(file.modified.date == timestamp.date)
modified.__tinydesc: Get/set the last time the file have been modified

temporary.__description: The File.temporary property set or get a boolean value indicating if the File is considered temporary or not (meaning that cache data will not be written to disk once the File is closed). The file must exist physically on the disk.
temporary.__example:
local file = sys.File("example\\notepad.wlua")
file.temporary = true

-- outputs "true"
print(file.temporary)
temporary.__tinydesc: Get/set the temporary status of the file

created.__description:
The File.created property set or get a Datetime value indicating when the file has been created. The file must exist physically on the disk.
If the file do not exist, this property returnsniland setting its value will have no effect.
created.__example:
local file = sys.File(sys.env["windir"].."\\win.ini")
print("Operating system was installed on "..file.created)
created.__tinydesc: Get/set the file creation date and time

size.__description: The File.size property returns a number representing the file size (in bytes) on disk.
size.__tinydesc: Get the File size on disk

accessed.__description:
The File.accessed property set or get a Datetime value indicating when the file has been last accessed. The file must exist physically on the disk.
If the file do not exist, this property returnsniland setting its value will have no effect.
accessed.__example:
local file = sys.File("newfile.txt"):open("write")
file:write("This is a test file")
file:close()

local timestamp = sys.Datetime("01/01/1970")
file.accessed = timestamp

-- outputs "true"
print(file.accessed == timestamp)
accessed.__tinydesc: Get/set last time the file was accessed

copy.__returns: Returns a boolean value indicating if the operation succeeded.
copy.__example:
local file = sys.File("test.txt"):open("write")
file:writeln("Hello World !")
file:close()

-- outputs false : cannot copy "test.txt" to "test.txt"
print(file:copy("test.txt"))

__params.__description:
A string representing the destination filename.
If the filename contains a path, the path must already exist. If the destination file exists, the function will fail.

copy.__description:
Copies the file physically on the disk to the specified filename.

Warning
copy.__tinydesc: Copy a file to a new one

File.__tinydesc: Object representation of a single file

write.__returns: Returns the number of bytes written to the file.
write.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

-- create a Buffer with bytes indicating "L��"
local msg = sys.Buffer("4CC3BBC3A4", "hex")

-- write the Buffer to the console.stdout standard File
console.stdout:write(msg)

__params.__description: A variable converted to a string value to be written to the file.

exists.__description: The File.exists property returns a boolean value indicating if the File exists physically on the drive or not.
exists.__example:
local file = sys.File("hello.txt")

if not file.exists then
	-- file does not exists, so we will create it
	file:open("write")
	file:write("Hello World !")
	file:close()
end
file:open()
print(file:read())

flush.__returns: This method returns no value.
flush.__description: Ensures that all recently written data is saved to the file.
flush.__tinydesc: Flush written data to the file

open.__returns: Returns the same File value if the operation succeeded or raises an error.
open.__example:
local file = sys.File("Hello.txt")

-- open the file for writing, using default "binary" encoding
file:open("write")

file:write("Hello World")
file:close()

__params.__description:
An optional string that indicates the kind of access when opening the file.

  • "read": the file will be opened for read operations, it's the default if neither mode nor encoding are specified.
  • "write": the file will be opened for write operations, erasing any data already present.
  • "append": the file will be opened for write operations, keeping the current data and starting writing at the end.
  • "readwrite": the file will be opened for read and write operations.

An optional string that indicates the encoding for read/write operations. By default, encoding is autodetected using Byte Mark Order (BOM) if not provided.

  • "binary" encoding : the file has no encoding, using raw bytes for read/write operations.
  • "utf8" encoding : the File is UTF8 encoded, using UTF8 characters for read/write operations.
  • "unicode" encoding : the File is UNICODE (UCS-2 LE) encoded, using UNICODE characters for read/write operations.
In "write" mode, Byte Order Mark (BOM) is written to the file when "utf8" or "unicode" encoding is provided.

open.__description: Open the file for read/write operations.
open.__tinydesc: Open the file for read/write/append operations.

move.__returns: Returns true if the operation succeeded or false otherwise (if a file is open in another program for example).
move.__example:
local file = sys.File("test.txt"):open("write")
file:writeln("Hello World !")
file:close()

-- rename file from "test.txt" to "hello.txt"
file:move("hello.txt")

__params.__description: A string representing the destination path.

move.__description: Moves the file physically on the disk to the specified path.
move.__tinydesc: Move the file to a new location/name

constructor.__description: The File constructor returns a File value, representing an abstraction of a file (that may or may not exist).

__params.__description:
A string representing the filename. The file may exist physically on disk or not. To create the file on a disk, use the File:open() method with "write" or "read/write" mode.

If a relative path is given to the File constructor, it will be converted to an absolute path (relative to the current working folder).

path.__description: The File.path property returns a string representing the path of the file (fullpath without the filename). The path is set during the call to the File constructor, relative to the current working directory at this time.
path.__example:
local file = sys.File("doc\\test.txt")

-- outputs the path, for example "C:\\luart\\doc\\"
print("file.path: "..file.path)

extension.__description: The File.extension property returns a string representing the extension of the File, including the "." character.

eof.__description: The File.eof property returns a boolean value indicating if the File position has reached the end of file or not. The file must have been opened before using the property.
eof.__example:
local file = sys.File("unicode.txt")
local msg = ""

file:open()

-- read the file char by char (slow ! do not use in real life !)
while not file.eof do
	msg = msg..file:read(1)
end

print(msg)
eof.__tinydesc: Check if end of file (EOF) position has been reached

copytask.__returns: Returns a Task that will return a boolean value indicating if the operation succeeded.
copytask.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

local file = sys.File("big_video.mkv")
file.step = 0

local function progress(total, done)
  local percentage = (done*100)/total
  if percentage / 10 > file.step then
    file.step = percentage / 10 + 1;
    console.write("■")
  end
end

console.write("Copying file... ")
await file:copy("big_video_copy.mkv", progress)

__params.__description:
A string representing the destination filename.
If the filename contains a path, the path must already exist. If the destination file exists, the function will fail.

An optional callback function that will be called periodically during the copy operation to get progress information. This function takes two parameters :

  • The first argument is the total bytes to be copied
  • The second argument is the number of bytes that have been copied

copytask.__description: Copies the file to a new one with the specified filename, asynchronously (without blocking program execution flow)
copytask.__tinydesc: Copy a file to a new one asynchronously

lines.__description: The File.lines iterator returns the next line in the File with each loop iteration. File should have been opened before in "read" mode. The line omits the leading end of line character marker.
lines.__example:
local file = sys.File("examples\\notepad.wlua")
	
file:open()
for line in file.lines do
	print(line)
end
lines.__tinydesc: Iterator over each line of the file

encoding.__description:
The File.encoding property returns the current file encoding. File should have been opened before to determine it's encoding or the property defaults to "binary" otherwise.
File encoding is one of those strings :
  • "binary" encoding : the File has no encoding, using raw bytes for read/write operations.
  • "utf8" encoding : the File is UTF8 encoded, using UTF8 characters for read/write operations.
  • "unicode" encoding : the File is UNICODE (UCS-2 LE) encoded, using UNICODE characters for read/write operations.
encoding.__example:
local file = sys.File("unicode.txt")
	
file:open("write", "unicode")
file:write("Hello World !")
file:close()
file:open("read")

-- outputs "unicode"
print(file.encoding)

readln.__returns: Returns the data as a string (for utf8 and unicode encoding) or as a Buffer (for binary encoding).
readln.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

console.write("What's you're name ? ")

-- read all characters from console.stdin until the key ENTER or RETURN is pressed
local name = console.stdin:readln()
print("Nice to meet you "..name.." !")
readln.__results: string or Buffer
readln.__description: Reads data from the file until end of line is encountered.
readln.__tinydesc: Read a line from the file

fullpath.__description: The File.fullpath property returns a string representing the full path of the File. The fullpath is set during the call to the File constructor, relative to the current working directory at this time.
fullpath.__example:
local file = sys.File("doc\\test.txt")

-- outputs the fullpath, for example "C:\\luart\\doc\\test.txt"
print("file.fullpath: "..file.fullpath)
fullpath.__tinydesc: Get full path of the file

fsentry.__returns: Returns a File or Directory instance that represents the filesystem entry. If the entry doesn't exists, it returns nil.
fsentry.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

local name = console.readln("Enter an entry name : ")
local entry = sys.fsentry(name)
if not entry then
  print("'"..name.."'".." doesn't exists !")
else
  print("'"..name.."'".." is a "..type(entry))
end
fsentry.__results: File | Directory

__params.__description: A string representing a filesystem entry name (ie a file name, or a folder name)

fsentry.__description: Get a filesystem entry by name or fullpath.

modified.__description: The Directory.modified property set or get a Datetime value indicating when the folder has been last modified.
modified.__example:
local windir = sys.env["windir"]
local dir = sys.Directory(windir)
print(windir.." has been modified on "..dir.modified)
modified.__tinydesc: Get/set last time the Directory was accessed

hidden.__description: The Directory.hidden property set or get a boolean value indicating if the folder is hidden or not. The folder must exist physically on the disk.
hidden.__example:
local dir = sys.Directory("hidden_dir\\")
dir:make()

dir.hidden = true

-- outputs "true"
print(dir.hidden)

Directory iteration.__description:
Directories are iterable through the Luart helper function each(), returning the next entry in the folder with each loop iteration. The folder must exist to iterate through its content.

Each iteration returns either a File or a Directory value
Directory iteration.__example:
-- iterate through all current directory content
for entry in each(sys.Directory(".")) do
	if type(entry) == "File" then
		print(entry.name)
	else -- entry is a directory
		print(entry.fullpath:match("\\.*\\([%w%p]+)$").."\\")
	end
end

name.__description: The Directory.name property returns a string representing the folder name (the last part of the fullpath).
name.__example:
local dir = sys.Directory(sys.currentdir)

-- outputs true
print(dir.parent.name == sys.Directory(sys.currentdir.."\\..").name)

move.__returns: Returns true if the operation succeeded or false otherwise (if a file is open in another program for example).
move.__example:
function rename_dir(oldpath, newpath)
	local dir = sys.Directory(oldpath)
	if dir.exists then
		return dir:move(newpath)
	end
	return false
end

-- create a "test" directory in current folder
sys.Directory("test"):make()

-- rename it to "newtest"
rename_dir("test", "newtest")

__params.__description: A string representing the destination path.

move.__description: Moves the folder physically on the disk and all contained files and subdirectories, to the specified path.
move.__tinydesc: Move the Directory to another location

parent.__description: The Directory.parent property returns a Directory representing the parent folder. If the folder is already a root folder, the property returns nil
parent.__example:
-- iterate through all current directory content
for entry in each(sys.Directory(".")) do
	if type(entry) == "File" then
		print(entry.name)
	else -- entry is a directory
		print(entry.fullpath:match("\\.*\\([%w%p]+)$").."\\")
	end
end

Directory.__description: The Directory object manages folders and their contents on disk

copy.__returns: Returns a boolean value indicating if the operation succeeded.
copy.__example:
local dir = sys.Directory(C:\\test")

-- copies the folder to another drive
print(dir:copy("D:\\test"))

__params.__description:
A string representing the destination directory.
If the dirname contains a path, the path must already exist.

copy.__description: Copies the Directory physically on the disk to the specified new directory name.
copy.__tinydesc: Copy a Directory to a new one

isempty.__description: The Directory.isempty property returns a boolean value indicating if the folder is empty (don't contains any file/directory) or not. If the folder don't exists, the property returns false
isempty.__example:
local tempdir = sys.Directory(sys.env.temp)
if tempdir.isempty then 
	print("Your temporary directory is not empty, you should think about to emptying it")
else
	print("Your temporary directory is empty")
end
isempty.__tinydesc: Check if the Directory is empty

exists.__description: The Directory.exists property returns a boolean value indicating if the folder exists physically on the drive or not.
exists.__example:
local SysWOW64 = sys.Directory(sys.env["windir"].."\\SysWOW64")
if SysWOW64.exists then 
	print("Windows 64 bits has been detected")
else
	print("Windows 32 bits has been detected")
end

list.__returns: Returns an iterator function to be used in a for loop, to iterate over the filtered directory content. Each iterator call returns either a File or a Directory value.
list.__example:
-- iterate through all current directory content
for entry in each(sys.Directory("."):list("*.*")) do
	if type(entry) == "File" then
		print(entry.name)
	else -- entry is a directory
		print(entry.fullpath:match("\\.*\\([%w%p]+)$").."\\")
	end
end

__params.__description: A string used to filter folder content. The string can include wildcard characters : an asterisk (*) for one or more characters march or a question mark (?) for single character match.

list.__description: Filters the folder content and returns an iterator.
list.__tinydesc: Iterate over a Directory using a filter

Directory.__tinydesc: Object for creating, moving, and enumerating through folders

make.__returns: Returns true if the operation succeeded or false otherwise (in case of an already existing folder for example).
make.__example:
local windir = sys.env["windir"]
local dir = sys.Directory(windir.."\\test")

-- cannot write to the Windows folder
if not dir:make() then
	print("Error accessing "..windir..": "..sys.error)
end
make.__description: Create a folder physically on the disk if it doesn't exists.
make.__tinydesc: Create the directory physically on the disk

remove.__returns: Returns true if the operation succeeded or false otherwise (in case of an non empty folder for example).
remove.__example:
-- outputs false, the current working directory could not be deleted !
print(sys.Directory(sys.currentdir):remove())
remove.__description: Deletes the folder physically on the disk if the folder is empty.
remove.__tinydesc: Delete the Directory from the disk

accessed.__description: The Directory.accessed property set or get a Datetime value indicating when the folder has been last accessed.
accessed.__example:
local windir = sys.env["windir"]
local dir = sys.Directory(windir)
print('Last access on directory "'..windir..'" was on '..dir.accessed)
accessed.__tinydesc: Get/set last time the Directory was accessed

copytask.__returns: Returns a Task that will return a boolean value indicating if the operation succeeded.
copytask.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

local dir = sys.Directory(C:\\test")

-- copies the folder to another drive without blocking
local task = dir:copy("D:\\test")

console.write("Copying directory... ")
while not task.terminated do
  console.write("■")
  sleep(1000)
end

__params.__description: A string representing the destination directory path.

copytask.__description: Copies the Directory to a new one with the specified folder path, asynchronously (without blocking program execution flow)
copytask.__tinydesc: Copy a Directory to a new one asynchronously

created.__description: The Directory.created property set or get a Datetime value indicating when the folder has been created.
created.__example:
local windir = sys.env["windir"]
local dir = sys.Directory(windir)
print("Operating system was created on "..dir.created)
created.__tinydesc: Get/set the Directory creation date and time

__params.__description:
A string representing the path to the folder. The path may exist physically on a disk or not. To create the folder on a disk, use the Directory:make() method.

If a relative path is given to the Directory constructor, it will be converted to an absolute path (relative to the current working folder).

local currentDir = sys.Directory(sys.currentdir)local tempDir = sys.Directory("temp")

constructor.__description: The Directory constructor returns a Directory value representing a folder (that may or may not exist on disk).
constructor.__example:
local currentDir = sys.Directory(sys.currentdir)
                    local tempDir = sys.Directory("temp")

removeall.__returns: Returns true if the operation succeeded or false otherwise (if a file is open in another program for example).
removeall.__example:
-- outputs false, the current working directory could not be deleted !
print(sys.Directory(sys.currentdir):removeall())
removeall.__description: Deletes the folder physically on the disk and all contained files and subdirectories.
removeall.__tinydesc: Delete the Directory with all its content

fullpath.__description: The Directory.fullpath property returns a string representing the full path to the folder. The fullpath is set during the call to the Directory constructor, relative to the current working directory at this time.
fullpath.__example:
local dir = sys.Directory(sys.currentdir)

-- outputs true
print(dir.parent.fullpath == sys.Directory(sys.currentdir.."\\..").fullpath)

tempdir.__returns: Returns a Directory Object that represents a temporary directory located in the Windows system temporary path. In case of error, this function returns nil
tempdir.__example:
local temp = sys.tempdir()
print("Temporary directory path: "..temp.fullpath)

__params.__description: An optional string that contains the first 3 letters of the temporary directory name.

tempdir.__description: Create a temporary empty Directory Object, created in the user temporary folder.
tempdir.__tinydesc: Generate a temporary Directory object in Windows temporary folder

read.__returns:
Returns a Task instance.
Once finished, the Task will return a string or nil if the executed process has been terminated.
read.__example:
local pipe = sys.Pipe('cmd /K dir /B')

-- Shows current directory content
local output = await(pipe:read())
print(output)

__params.__description: An optional number representing the delay to wait before reading, in milliseconds, defaulting to 100

read.__description: Reads from the Pipe (from the stdout and stderr of the executed process).
read.__tinydesc: Read data asynchronously from the pipe (from associated process output stream)

close.__example:
local pipe = sys.Pipe("cmd.exe /C SET")

-- list all current environment variables in the env_vars table
sleep(200)
local output = await(pipe:read())
local env_vars = {}

pipe:close()

for var, value in output:gmatch("(%w+)=(.-)\r\n") do
  env_vars[var] = value
    print("Variable `"..var.."` : \t", value)
end
close.__description: Closes the Pipe and the associated process. Any further read/write/readerror operations won't achieve.

__params.__description: A string representing the command to execute.

constructor.__description: The Pipe constructor executes a process and returns a Pipe value to interact with it.
constructor.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

-- executes "cmd.exe" and create a Pipe
local pipe = sys.Pipe("cmd.exe")
-- skip cmd.exe welcome message
await(pipe:read())
pipe:write("dir\n")

--read the result of the "dir" command excluding the command prompt
result = await(pipe:read()):match(".-\n(.*)\n.-\n")
print(result)

write.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import console

-- executes "cmd.exe" and create a Pipe
local pipe = sys.Pipe("cmd.exe")
-- skip cmd.exe welcome message
await(pipe:read())
pipe:write("dir\n")

--read the result of the "dir" command excluding the command prompt
result = await(pipe:read()):match(".-\n(.*)\n.-\n")
print(result)

__params.__description: A variable converted to a string value to be written to the Pipe.

write.__description: Writes data to the Pipe (into the standard input of the executed process).
write.__tinydesc: Write data to the pipe (towards associated process input stream)

Pipe.__description: The Pipe object lets you run another program and exchange data with it through its input and output streams
Pipe.__tinydesc: Manage redirection with another process, for standard input/output

currentdir.__description: The sys.currentdir property get or set a string representing the current working directory.
currentdir.__example:
-- check if the subdirectory \log exists in current working directory				
local dir = sys.Directory(sys.currentdir.."\\log")
if not dir.exists then
	error("Directory "..sys.currentdir.."\\log does not exist")
end
